jokes.text: Oh, there's a woman in here Who couldn't afford a Persian rug, so she painted one on her tiled floor. The magazine is celebrating her ingenuity! Well, it sounds like a creative solution. A creative solution would be a lobotomy to make one forget they like nice things.
jokes.setup: Magazine celebrates woman's creative solution of painting a rug on floor
jokes.punchline: Moira suggests a lobotomy would be more creative solution
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moira character moment - her dramatic overreaction to middle-class solutions is both shocking and perfectly in character. The escalation from 'creative solution' to 'lobotomy' is expertly structured.

jokes.text: As if you think that's what I listen to!
jokes.setup: Johnny asks David to turn down the music
jokes.punchline: David's offended denial that he'd listen to Melissa Manchester
jokes.explanation: Good character beat for David's pretentious music taste, but fairly standard sitcom fare.

jokes.text: The entire Rose family chaos with banging, yelling, and confusion over loud music
jokes.setup: Music is too loud and family tries to get neighbor to turn it down
jokes.punchline: Complete breakdown into overlapping shouting match
jokes.explanation: Classic sitcom chaos but runs a bit long and relies on volume over wit.

jokes.text: Moira's excessive sighing sequence
jokes.setup: Morning after sleepless night
jokes.punchline: Moira dramatically sighs multiple times to wake Johnny
jokes.explanation: Visual gag showing Moira's dramatics, but fairly predictable character beat.

jokes.text: No, they look like they usually look, Moira. So now my eyes are usually puffy!
jokes.setup: Moira asks if her eyes look puffy from lack of sleep
jokes.punchline: Johnny's reassurance backfires spectacularly
jokes.explanation: Classic married couple miscommunication with perfect escalation. Johnny's well-meaning response creates worse problem.

jokes.text: These bags are like gypsy caravans, packed to the brim!
jokes.setup: Moira lamenting her under-eye bags
jokes.punchline: Elaborate metaphor comparing them to gypsy caravans
jokes.explanation: Peak Moira dialogue - completely over-the-top metaphor that's both absurd and strangely poetic. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: Roland! What are you doing here? Well, we're, uh, doing some renos on the uh, house, so we're gonna shack up here until they're all done.
jokes.setup: Johnny discovers Roland is the source of the noise
jokes.punchline: Roland casually reveals he's staying next door
jokes.explanation: Standard plot setup joke. The horror for Johnny is clear but not particularly witty.

jokes.text: Now, whatever you and the missus do after hours, that's your business, not mine. On the walls Roland, with our fists! And if you're into that kind of stuff, that doesn't bother me at all.
jokes.setup: Johnny mentions 'banging away' on the walls
jokes.punchline: Roland's sexual misinterpretation despite Johnny's clarification
jokes.explanation: Decent misdirection joke that plays on Roland's obliviousness, but relies on innuendo.

jokes.text: You said 'renovations.' Yes... renovations, because our basement flooded!
jokes.setup: Johnny catches Roland in inconsistent story
jokes.punchline: Roland's defensive justification that makes no sense
jokes.explanation: Roland's inability to maintain a simple lie is character-appropriate but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: I mean living in a space this limited, is trying enough on a marriage without having Roland wallowing next door. This is going to bring us to a breaking point!
jokes.setup: Moira worried about marriage stress from Roland
jokes.punchline: The irony of blaming external forces for marriage issues
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Moira's tendency to externalize problems, sets up the coming argument nicely.

jokes.text: All right, John. I... I drink a little too much tea sometimes. And that's it for you? You uh... You drink tea?
jokes.setup: Moira admits her own flaw - drinking too much tea
jokes.punchline: Johnny's incredulity at this minor 'flaw'
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Moira's complete lack of self-awareness about her actual flaws vs. minor tea drinking.

jokes.text: That would only be after you've taken that condescending father tone with me. Father tone? There it is.
jokes.setup: Moira accuses Johnny of having a condescending father tone
jokes.punchline: Johnny immediately proves her point by using that exact tone
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta moment where Johnny proves Moira's point in real-time. Perfect structure and character work.

jokes.text: But only when you act like a big child! I act like a child? Yes John, and now is not the time for you to throw one of your tantrums!
jokes.setup: Moira calls Johnny childish
jokes.punchline: Her increasingly hysterical tone while accusing him of tantrums
jokes.explanation: Good escalation in the argument with nice irony as Moira becomes increasingly dramatic while calling Johnny childish.

jokes.text: That's another thing Mr. Rose, you never finish your fights. Oh, this one's finished!
jokes.setup: Moira accuses Johnny of never finishing fights
jokes.punchline: Johnny's immediate contradiction by storming out
jokes.explanation: Nice button to the argument where Johnny proves Moira's point yet again by walking out.

jokes.text: Stevie, you look like you would know about affordable housing.
jokes.setup: Alexis needs advice about apartment hunting
jokes.punchline: Her assumption that Stevie would know about cheap housing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Alexis moment - casually insulting while asking for help, completely oblivious to the rudeness.

jokes.text: I wouldn't really describe any apartment as cute in this town.
jokes.setup: Alexis asks if an apartment is cute
jokes.punchline: Stevie's dry dismissal of the entire town's housing
jokes.explanation: Good Stevie deadpan delivery that establishes the town's limitations while deflating Alexis's enthusiasm.

jokes.text: There's a collection of undershirts hang drying outside of my room. Is there any way they could be removed, or is there like, a Texas Chainsaw movie being filmed out there that I'm not aware of?
jokes.setup: David complains about Roland's laundry
jokes.punchline: Comparing hanging undershirts to a horror movie set
jokes.explanation: Perfect David hyperbole and pop culture reference. His dramatic reaction to mundane laundry is very character-appropriate.

jokes.text: I am thinking about getting a place. I have some money coming in, and it's just... It's not really a cute look to be this age, and live at home. No offense, David.
jokes.setup: Alexis explains why she wants to move out
jokes.punchline: Casually insulting David while making her point
jokes.explanation: Typical Alexis casual cruelty masked as innocent observation. Good sibling dynamic.

jokes.text: M'kay well, you're a full grown man, who's much older than me, living at home. So it's just a little bit embarrassing.
jokes.setup: David's defensive response to her first insult
jokes.punchline: Alexis doubling down with more specific insults
jokes.explanation: Continuation of the sibling dynamic, escalating the insult with specificity.

jokes.text: Because David, as an attractive, single female, I don't feel comfortable going to look at an apartment by myself!
jokes.setup: David questions why he needs to help apartment hunt
jokes.punchline: Alexis's self-aggrandizing reason for needing protection
jokes.explanation: Classic Alexis self-centeredness disguised as vulnerability. Her casual self-promotion is in character.

jokes.text: Mmmm... that person doesn't exist in this town.
jokes.setup: Alexis suggests the realtor might be cute
jokes.punchline: David's definitive dismissal of attractive men in town
jokes.explanation: Good callback to David's standards and the town's limitations. Economical and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Sad sack-y today. Sad sack-y?
jokes.setup: Jocelyn observes Johnny's mood
jokes.punchline: Johnny's confusion at her made-up descriptor
jokes.explanation: Jocelyn's unique vocabulary creating a moment of confusion. Mildly amusing character beat.

jokes.text: That's where we had our honeymoon. Had a lot of great times in that room.
jokes.setup: Jocelyn mentions Roland might end up at the motel
jokes.punchline: Oversharing about their honeymoon in that exact room
jokes.explanation: Jocelyn's inappropriate oversharing creates cringe humor. Johnny's discomfort is palpable.

jokes.text: It was actually the first song that we ever made... Okay, I really don't need the details on that.
jokes.setup: Jocelyn about to share more intimate details
jokes.punchline: Johnny's horrified interruption
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment with Johnny's panic at potential TMI. The misdirection works well.

jokes.text: Well, it's kinda obvious that you and Moira are a little bit rocky right now.
jokes.setup: Johnny denies any marriage problems
jokes.punchline: Jocelyn's blunt observation of the obvious
jokes.explanation: Straightforward observational humor about Johnny's denial. Not particularly clever but functional.

jokes.text: This is so cute for me. This is cute, right? I think this is super cute. Wait, is it cute? 'Cause I know that it's light, and spacious, and... airy in here, but I just wasn't sure if it was cute. Oh, it's cute.
jokes.setup: Alexis viewing the apartment
jokes.punchline: Her obsessive need for validation about 'cuteness'
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Alexis's need for constant validation, though it runs a bit long.

jokes.text: Yes, but we can rip those out.
jokes.setup: David asks about original moldings
jokes.punchline: Ray's willingness to destroy potential selling point
jokes.explanation: Good Ray moment showing his complete lack of sales skills or appreciation for value.

jokes.text: Yes, we found an incinerated squirrel carcass there last week.
jokes.setup: Alexis asks if fireplace is functioning
jokes.punchline: Ray's matter-of-fact mention of dead squirrel
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor delivered with Ray's characteristic obliviousness to how disturbing this information is.

jokes.text: Wait, $275 a month for this? That's cheaper than my P.O. Box in New York.
jokes.setup: Learning the rent price
jokes.punchline: David's perspective on NYC costs vs. small town
jokes.explanation: Good fish-out-of-water humor highlighting the economic culture clash between NYC and small town.

jokes.text: it actually kind of reminds me of your old um... office space that you had in the West Village.
jokes.setup: Looking at basic apartment
jokes.punchline: Comparing it to David's former trendy NYC office
jokes.explanation: Good sibling needle - Alexis knows exactly how to highlight David's fall from grace.

jokes.text: No. I was gonna say sensitive. Oh, sensitive. Especially when I wanna do something on my own. Ohhh! Do you really think he's obnoxious and irritating?
jokes.setup: Johnny assumes Jocelyn will call Roland irritating
jokes.punchline: She was going to say 'sensitive' then gets excited by Johnny's words
jokes.explanation: Nice misdirection where Jocelyn subverts expectations, then gets distracted by Johnny's harsh words.

jokes.text: You are so right! You know, I thought that I might be ready to forgive him, but I think he needs a couple more nights at that motel.
jokes.setup: Johnny accidentally encourages Jocelyn's anger
jokes.punchline: She decides to punish Roland longer based on Johnny's venting
jokes.explanation: Good irony as Johnny's complaints backfire, extending his own problem. Shows consequences of venting.

jokes.text: But, on the other hand... maybe it's through your forgiveness that you teach him a lesson. Right, and... and the sooner you forgive him, then... then the better it's gonna be for... for you.
jokes.setup: Johnny realizes he made a mistake encouraging Jocelyn's anger
jokes.punchline: His transparent attempt to backtrack and get Roland back home
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment as Johnny desperately tries to undo his mistake. His transparent self-interest is obvious.

jokes.text: Close the door honey, will you? You're lettin' my flies out.
jokes.setup: Moira enters Roland's motel room
jokes.punchline: Roland's concern about escaping flies
jokes.explanation: Perfect Roland line - disgusting, absurd, and delivered casually. Shows his complete lack of shame about living conditions.

jokes.text: I'm sorry, are you complaining about the quality of the free lodging that I arranged for you this past year?
jokes.setup: Moira complains about Roland's loud music
jokes.punchline: Roland's indignant response about providing 'free lodging'
jokes.explanation: Good Roland moment showing his ability to twist any situation to make himself the victim/hero.

jokes.text: (Imitating Moira) Hello, everyone! I'm Moira Rose, and I know famous people, so I really can't talk to the common folk. Excuse me all, coming through!
jokes.setup: Roland and Moira's escalating argument
jokes.punchline: Roland's crude but accurate impression of Moira
jokes.explanation: Roland's impression is crude but hits close enough to home to be effective. Physical comedy implied.

jokes.text: The apartment was soundproofed by the previous owner. But the irony is the people next door are completely deaf, so you could literally scream for hours, and no one would hear you. Did you hear that? You could scream for hours, and no one would hear you.
jokes.setup: Ray explaining the apartment's soundproofing
jokes.punchline: The ominous implications he's completely oblivious to
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor with Ray being completely oblivious to how sinister this sounds. David's reaction makes it even funnier.

jokes.text: Yes, someone did take their own life in this space, but the good news is, we've replaced the ceiling fan, it's now a break-away model.
jokes.setup: David and Alexis hesitant about the apartment
jokes.punchline: Ray's casual mention of suicide with 'helpful' improvement
jokes.explanation: Peak dark comedy delivered with Ray's characteristic obliviousness. The 'good news' framing is perfectly absurd and horrifying.

jokes.text: You are an emotionally stunted, self absorbed man-child. You lord this counterfeit power of yours over others like a schoolyard bully!
jokes.setup: Roland and Moira's argument reaching peak intensity
jokes.punchline: Moira's elaborate insults delivered with theatrical intensity
jokes.explanation: Excellent Moira vocabulary deployed as weapons. The theatrical delivery and specific insults are very character-appropriate.

jokes.text: You're just a stuck up pencil skirt with clown makeup, and a janitor's mop on your head!
jokes.setup: Roland responding to Moira's elaborate insults
jokes.punchline: His crude but visually accurate description of Moira
jokes.explanation: Roland's insult is crude but devastatingly accurate about Moira's appearance. The contrast with her verbose style is perfect.

jokes.text: Your marriage is as blighted as this town, and you have no one to blame but yourself! Whoa... Well, hold on. I went too far.
jokes.setup: The insult fight reaching its peak
jokes.punchline: Both suddenly realizing the line was crossed
jokes.explanation: Good moment where the comedy fight becomes real. The sudden deflation works well structurally.

jokes.text: You know, sometimes at night, I see things. Oh my God, David! Like... dark things. Like sometimes there's this old woman that paces back and forth by your bed, waving her hands over your face. I don't think her bony fingers ever touch your mouth, though.
jokes.setup: David making up ghost stories to scare Alexis
jokes.punchline: Increasingly specific and creepy details
jokes.explanation: Good sibling torture with David's creative ghost story. The specific details make it more effective.

jokes.text: Do you ever wake up with chapped lips? Dad! Dad's dead. Ohh, you're such a dick, David!
jokes.setup: David continuing the ghost story with chapped lips detail
jokes.punchline: Fake death revelation and Alexis's exasperated response
jokes.explanation: Continuation of the ghost story prank with good escalation to fake death. Typical sibling dynamics.
